summary.intent: Implement the Group agnostic core component (task #205) in the ars-ui Rust component library, then improve the development workflow by adding a Codex review-wait skill and a faster local CI profile (`cargo xci --profile fast`).
summary.outcome: PR #648 was opened with the full Group component implementation, passed three Codex review passes (fixing `aria-readonly` root emission and stale doc comment), all CI gates passed except an unrelated flaky DOM Browser animation test. The `waiting-for-codex-review` skill and `cargo xci --profile fast` (1m45s vs 25min full) were implemented, committed, and pushed to the same PR.

repo: `aria-readonly` is NOT a WAI-ARIA 1.2 global state attribute — it is only valid on specific roles (checkbox, textbox, combobox, grid, etc.), unlike `aria-disabled` and `aria-invalid` which are global and valid on any role including `role=presentation`.
`CLAUDE.md` is a symlink to `AGENTS.md`; edits must target `AGENTS.md` directly.
`.claude/skills/` is a symlink that resolves to `.agents/skills/` — repo-local skills live in `.agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`.
The spec is the authoritative contract (CLAUDE.md 'spec is the contract'); GitHub issue test bullets that contradict the spec must be dropped in favor of spec-aligned tests.
Secondary context structs must use descriptive non-prefixed names per foundation 08 §8.4 (e.g., `GroupContext` not `Context`) so consumers write `group::GroupContext`.
Snapshots must be reviewed before committing and the PR labeled `snapshot-reviewed` per CLAUDE.md.
Issues must be moved to 'In Progress' on the project board before starting implementation.

`cargo xci` runs ~25 minutes locally due to wasm `-Cinstrument-coverage` recompile under nightly + LLVM 22; the new `cargo xci --profile fast` skips Coverage and all five FeatureMatrix steps and completes in ~1m45s.
`post-implementation-audit` skill is mandatory per CLAUDE.md §7 before committing; covers spec/impl drift, iterative gap rounds (≥2 clean), and mutation + coverage audit.
Spec-conformance tests live in `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/utility.rs` and require a `{component}_anatomy_matches_spec` entry for each new component.
The `cargo-mutants` 'unviable' category includes `const fn` and builder-return-self mutations that can't compile — these are a correctness signal, not a gap.
Codex's bot login on this install is `chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]` — important for reaction/review polling scripts.

code.finding: Template file for all new stateless utility components — identical structure to Group minus the Context struct.
Reference pattern for a secondary context struct with propagation fields.
Step enum and PIPELINE_ORDER constant — new `Profile { Full, Fast }` enum and `FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER` (10 steps) were added alongside these. `resolve_steps` now accepts a `Profile` argument.
Existing tests module starts here; 7 new profile/resolve_steps tests were added at the bottom.
`Command::Ci` variant — `--profile <full|fast>` flag added here with `default_value_t = ci::Profile::Full` for backward compat.
`Direction::as_html_attr` implementation — used by stateless utility components to emit `dir` HTML attribute.
Foundation §8.4 naming rule for secondary contexts: use descriptive non-prefixed names like `GroupContext`, not bare `Context`.

workflow: TDD is enforced by workspace convention: failing tests must be written first, confirmed red, before the implementation fix lands.

The `Monitor` tool can drop intermediate events — for terminal-verdict polling (waiting for approval/rejection), use `Bash run_in_background` with a polling loop instead; it gives a single reliable completion notification.
Codex signals completion by *dropping* the 👀 reaction: replaced by 👍 for approval, or simply removed when review threads are posted. 👍 is never added alongside threads.
After pushing a PR and after each subsequent fix push, post `@codex review` as a top-level comment — auto-retrigger fires sometimes but is not reliable.
The initial `@codex review` comment should be posted immediately after PR creation, before any polling.
Pre-push gate: run `cargo xci --profile fast` (~1.5 min); reserve full `cargo xci` (~25 min) for the final pre-PR run or after substantial refactors that may shift feature-flag interactions.
`cargo insta test --workspace -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` is the correct snapshot orphan gate — running it with a file filter causes other crates' snapshots to appear unreferenced.
Pre-existing doc warnings (e.g., `cargo doc` failures at unrelated line 749) should be verified against a baseline before attributing them to new changes.
When a spec has internal contradictions, resolve on accessibility/correctness merits and update the spec prose to match the best-outcome implementation in the same PR.

friction: Spec §3.1 prose contradicted the §1.2 code example on whether state attributes should be emitted with `role=presentation` — required a planning-phase resolution and user confirmation before implementation.
GitHub issue #205 listed two test bullets (`label`/`label_id` props) that directly contradicted the spec's Props struct — required dropping them with explicit justification.
Spec wrote `Api<'a> { props: &'a Props }` (borrowed lifetime) but the workspace convention and adapter sketches both assumed an owned `Api` — required a Phase 1 audit finding to correct the spec.
`cargo xci` took ~25 minutes locally, making it impractical as a routine pre-push gate — drove the `--profile fast` work.
The `Monitor` tool dropped the 👍 approval notification after the third Codex pass; the approval had already been posted minutes earlier and was only discovered by direct state inspection.

Codex's second review pass caught the `aria-readonly` global/role-supported boundary mistake — a real ARIA spec error in the initial implementation that required a TDD red-phase fix, snapshot update, and spec prose update.
Codex's third pass caught a stale doc comment on `Props::read_only` that still described the removed `aria-readonly` emission.
DOM Browser Tests failed in CI on the final push due to two flaky animation-timing tests in `ars-dom` that are unrelated to any changed code — unresolved at end of session.
open_items: Leptos adapter Group component (`ars-leptos`) — deliberately deferred to a separate task on the project board.
Dioxus adapter Group component (`ars-dioxus`) — deliberately deferred to a separate task.
Wiring `GroupContext` consumption into existing child components (Button, TextField, etc.) — deferred; requires adapter components to exist first.
DOM Browser Tests CI failure on PR #648 — two flaky animation-timing tests in `crates/ars-dom/platform::wasm_tests` unrelated to #205 changes; needs investigation.
`--exclude <step>` flag for ad-hoc subtraction from a CI profile — deferred as a follow-up to the `--profile fast` work.
Pre-push git hook that auto-runs `cargo xci-fast` — deferred; enforcement currently relies on CLAUDE.md/habit.
Potentially flipping the no-arg `cargo xci` default from `full` to `fast` — deferred pending telemetry on actual local usage patterns.

The `waiting-for-codex-review` skill's polling section could be strengthened: after a Monitor-based wait, always do a direct `gh api` state check before concluding nothing happened, since Monitor notifications are not guaranteed to be delivered.
